hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (e.g., W, F, Y) will enhance complementarity with SSTR2-specific ECL2/TM5 residues (e.g., 193, 205), while mutating position 11 to hydrophobic residues (e.g., L, I, V) will improve steric fit in the SSTR2-specific TM6 pocket (272/273), thereby increasing selectivity margin over off-targets.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current hypothesis remains valid: the design strategy is producing highly selective SSTR2 binders, but structural modeling limitations are preventing the generation of high-quality candidates. Focusing on improving structural modeling parameters will help retain selectivity while improving model quality.

proposed_changes.rationale: Increase the number of candidates to improve the chances of generating structurally valid models with high pLDDT and docking scores.
Reduce the clash threshold to enforce better packing and reduce structural failures due to poor geometry.
